reviewNotes: Comprehensive contract covering all three sprint features (F8: Game State Machine, F9: HUD, F10: Menu System) with 28 well-structured criteria. Criteria are testable, specific, and have clear thresholds. Out-of-scope items are correctly deferred to future sprints (World Map to Sprint 4, enemies/bosses to Sprint 5, audio to Sprint 10). Regression coverage of Sprints 1 and 2 is included both as explicit criteria (C-27, C-28) and via regressionSprints field.

criteriaAssessment.assessment: GOOD. Covers 5 states (TITLE, STAGE, PAUSED, GAME_OVER, STAGE_COMPLETE) which matches what Sprint 3 needs. WORLD_MAP, BOSS, and VICTORY correctly deferred. The 'at least' phrasing allows extensibility. Test steps verify clean transition functions and proper initialization — not just enum definitions.
GOOD. Testable via Playwright screenshot. Verifies game opens to title (not gameplay), game name is visible, and player is NOT active. Clear threshold.
GOOD. Animation test via dual screenshots at T and T+500ms is a valid approach for canvas games. Threshold accepts multiple animation styles (pulsing, blinking, fading).
GOOD. Dual screenshot comparison with 2-second gap will catch any animated background. Threshold is flexible (scrolling, particles, or other motion).
GOOD. Tests the critical title-to-gameplay transition. Verifies player appears AND responds to input after transition. 2-second wait for transition animation is sensible given iris-wipe.
GOOD. Tests controls screen access and content. Key binding list covers all Sprint 2 inputs plus Escape for pause.
GOOD. Verifies round-trip: title -> controls -> title -> gameplay still works. Prevents dead-end state.
GOOD. Verifies both visual (overlay) and functional (gameplay frozen) aspects of pausing.
GOOD. Three options (Resume, Restart, Quit to Title) correctly omits Return to World Map which is Sprint 4. Verifies default selection highlight.
GOOD. Tests two resume paths (menu selection and Escape key). Verifies gameplay actually resumes.
GOOD. Comprehensive restart test: position, health, and level state reset. Specific about 3 HP restoration.
GOOD. Verifies full round-trip back to a functional title screen from gameplay via pause menu.
GOOD. Keyboard navigation is critical for a game with no mouse. Tests Up/Down arrows and Enter activation.
GOOD. Tests initial state (3 filled hearts) AND damage state (2 filled + 1 empty). Aligns with design spec (Ember Red filled, Steel Blue empty outline).
GOOD. Coin icon + counter at 0. Correctly sets expectation that coins don't increment yet (Sprint 4/5).
GOOD. Stage name in top-right. Flexible about exact text format.
GOOD. Matches design spec requirement for Deep Charcoal semi-transparent backgrounds. Tests readability, which is the design principle.

ACCEPTABLE with note. Test steps mention 'via debug API or repeated deaths' for reaching Game Over. The generator should ensure the debug API exposes methods to set lives/HP directly (e.g., window.__gameDebug.setLives(1), setHP(1)) to make this testable without tediously dying 15 times. C-24 covers debug API for game state but not player stats — the evaluator may need to chain through debug API.
GOOD. Verifies Continue restores full HP AND lives. Specific about expected values (3 HP, 5 lives).
GOOD. Verifies Game Over -> Title transition.

ACCEPTABLE. Debug API trigger for stage completion is pragmatic since real level exits don't exist yet. Verifies time and coins display. Note: does not specify what the 'continue' action does (go to title? next stage?). This is fine since the next stage/world map doesn't exist yet — returning to title is the expected behavior.
GOOD but potentially flaky. Rapid screenshot capture (every 100ms) is ambitious but valid for Playwright. C-23 provides code-review backup for the same feature, which is smart.
GOOD. Code-review complement to C-22. Verifies two-phase close/open, midpoint state change, configurable duration, and input blocking. Thorough.
GOOD. Debug API testability is essential. Tests initial state = TITLE and transition to STAGE. Should ideally also expose player HP/lives for C-18 testing, but this is an implicit requirement.
GOOD. Code-review for boss health bar component is appropriate since no bosses exist yet. Verifies conditional rendering, name label, and proportional fill.
GOOD. Architecture criterion ensures HUD and menus are modular. Prevents monolithic game.js bloat.
GOOD. Comprehensive regression covering movement, combat, health/lives, camera, parallax, hazards. 9 test steps cover the most critical Sprint 1 & 2 mechanics.
GOOD. Canvas resolution, FPS, and zero console errors. Standard regression baseline.

coverageAnalysis.F8_GameStateMachine: FULLY COVERED by C-01 (state definitions), C-05 (title->stage transition), C-08 (stage->paused), C-10 (paused->stage), C-12 (paused->title), C-18 (stage->gameover), C-19 (gameover->stage), C-20 (gameover->title), C-22/C-23 (iris-wipe transitions), C-24 (debug API).
coverageAnalysis.F9_HUD: FULLY COVERED by C-14 (health hearts), C-15 (coin counter), C-16 (stage name), C-17 (visual styling), C-25 (boss health bar code).
coverageAnalysis.F10_MenuSystem: FULLY COVERED by C-02/C-03/C-04 (title screen), C-05 (start game), C-06/C-07 (controls screen), C-08/C-09/C-10/C-11/C-12/C-13 (pause menu), C-18/C-19/C-20 (game over screen), C-21 (stage complete screen).

minorGaps: Game Over and Stage Complete screens lack an explicit keyboard navigation criterion (C-13 only covers pause menu). The evaluator should verify arrow key navigation works on all menu screens during testing.
C-24 (debug API) should ideally also expose player HP/lives setters for practical Game Over testing (C-18). This is implicit but not stated.

No criterion explicitly verifies design spec colors (Ember Red #D94F4F for hearts, Muted Gold #C4A35A for coins, Deep Charcoal #1A1A2E for backgrounds). C-17 tests the concept but not the specific palette.
outOfScopeReview: Correctly defers: World Map (Sprint 4), Save/Load (Sprint 4), Coins (Sprint 4/5), Enemies/Bosses (Sprint 5), Audio (Sprint 10), Advanced VFX (Sprint 11), Victory screen (Sprint 9+), Return to World Map in pause menu (Sprint 4). All deferrals align with sprint plan dependencies.
regressionReview: regressionSprints correctly lists [1, 2]. C-27 and C-28 provide inline regression checks. The evaluator will also re-run Sprint 1 and Sprint 2 criteria from the regression registry for full coverage.
